title: Talking about everyday life
objectives: Use the simple present to talk about habits and routines
Add -s/-es for he, she, and it
Form simple negative and question sentences
intro: The simple present is how we talk about things that are generally true, and about habits and routines we repeat. Let's learn it step by step.

concept.title: What is the simple present?
concept.explanation:
We use the simple present for habits, routines, and facts — things that are true in general, not just right now.

The basic structure is:
Subject + base verb (+ object).
examples: The sun rises in the east.
concept.note: Signal words like always, usually, often, every day, and never often point to the simple present.

assignment.title: Quick check: habits & routines
assignment.intro: Let's make sure the basics are clear.

questions.prompt: Which sentence describes a daily habit?
options: I am eating lunch right now.
I eat lunch at one o'clock every day.
I ate lunch an hour ago.
questions.hint: Look for a routine that repeats, not a single moment.
questions.feedback: "Every day" signals a repeated habit — that's the simple present.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: They ____ (play) football on Sundays.
questions.feedback: With they, the base verb stays the same: they play.

concept.title: He, she, it: add -s
concept.explanation:
For he, she, and it, we add -s (or -es) to the verb.

I/you/we/they work → he/she/it works.
examples: He watches TV in the evening.
It rains a lot in July.
concept.note: Verbs ending in -ch, -sh, -ss, -x, or -o usually take -es: watch → watches, go → goes.

questions.prompt: Complete: My brother ____ (go) to school by bus.
Put the words in order: (English / she / speaks / well)
questions.feedback: Subject + verb(+s) + object/adverb: She speaks English well.

assignment.title: Final check
assignment.intro: A short mixed review to finish the lesson.

questions.hint: After doesn't, use the base verb.
questions.feedback: Negatives for he/she/it use doesn't + base verb: She doesn't like coffee.

questions.prompt: Write 2–3 sentences about your daily routine using the simple present.
